risk_domain.name: L2 Anonymity Set Monitoring
risk_domain.description: Monitors privacy degradation on Layer 2 rollups where faster block times, sequencer centralization, and rollup batch posting create deanonymization vectors absent on L1

capabilities: full L2 transaction observation including ordering within batches
L1 batch posting observation and timing analysis
cross-layer correlation between L1 and L2 transactions
timing analysis at sub-second granularity on L2
forced inclusion transaction monitoring on L1
limitations: no access to sequencer internal state (unless sequencer is the adversary)
no ability to modify L2 state without valid transactions

H1_thin_privacy_pool.name: thin privacy pool
H1_thin_privacy_pool.description: Few unique depositors or matches in the current pool, reducing anonymity.

signals.name: few_unique_depositors
signals.description: Few unique depositors within the user's denomination bracket over a certain period.

signals.name: sparse_match_count
signals.description: Deposit amount has few matches in the current pool, indicating reduced anonymity.

signals.name: timed_lag_deposit
signals.description: No other deposits occurred within a specified time frame after the user's deposit in the same denomination bracket.

recommendations.description: Mix assets across different addresses before depositing.
Use a multisignature wallet for transactions to spread out activity.
Perform token swaps between different tokens within the same blockchain.
Utilize a cryptocurrency mixing service to enhance privacy.
Wait for a random period before making deposits.

H2_sequencer_visibility.name: sequencer visibility
H2_sequencer_visibility.description: Centralized sequencer provides full transaction content visibility without encryption.

signals.name: single_sequencer
signals.description: Identifies presence of a single centralized sequencer.

signals.name: unencrypted_mempool
signals.description: Detects the lack of encryption in the mempool or transaction content.

signals.name: ip_correlation
signals.description: Looks for user activity from same IP address, indicating potential sequencer-side correlation.

signals.name: rpc_endpoint_reuse
signals.description: Detects usage of the same RPC endpoint by a user, which could enable sequencer to correlate transactions.

recommendations.description: Enable encryption for mempool and transaction content.
Implement a decentralized sequencer system to reduce single point of failure.
Ensure users switch IPs for each transaction to avoid IP correlation.
Periodically change the RPC endpoint used by clients.
Add redundancy to network nodes to avoid relying on a single sequencer.

H3_forced_inclusion_deanonymizati.name: forced inclusion deanonymization
H3_forced_inclusion_deanonymizati.description: Use of L1 forced inclusion for privacy-sensitive transactions leaks user identity.

signals.name: forced_inclusion
signals.description: Transaction submitted via L1 forced inclusion rather than the L2 sequencer.

signals.name: linkable_address
signals.description: The L1 address submitting the transaction is associated with user's identity.

signals.name: rare_transaction
signals.description: Transaction is a rare forced inclusion, making it stand out.

recommendations.description: Disable forced L1 inclusion for privacy-sensitive transactions.
Implement zero-knowledge proofs to obfuscate transaction details.
Anonymize the L1 address used for transactions.
Distribute privacy-sensitive transactions across multiple addresses.

H4_l2_specific_timing_correlation.name: L2-specific timing correlation
H4_l2_specific_timing_correlation.description: Short time between deposit and withdrawal on the same chain limits anonymity.

signals.name: short_deposit_withdraw_gap
signals.description: Detects short time interval between deposit and withdraw on the same L2 chain.

signals.name: tight_timing_correlation
signals.description: Identifies deposits and withdrawals occurring within a specific timeframe (e.g., <100 blocks or seconds) on the same L2 chain.

signals.name: unique_timing_gap
signals.description: Checks if the time gap between a deposit and withdraw is unique within the pool of transactions on the same L2 chain.

recommendations.description: Monitor for short deposit-withdrawal intervals to detect suspicious activity.
Delay deposits or withdrawals by a random amount of time to obscure timing patterns.
Use blockchain mixing services that anonymize transactions by pooling them with others.

H5_rollup_batch_linkage.name: rollup batch linkage
H5_rollup_batch_linkage.description: Transactions appearing in close batches on L1 reduce overall anonymity.

signals.name: batch_size_small
signals.description: Identifies unusually small batches containing user transactions.

signals.name: same_batch_transactions
signals.description: Detects deposits and withdrawals appearing in the same L1 batch posting.

signals.name: adjacent_batches
signals.description: Checks if a deposit and withdrawal are within adjacent batches.

recommendations.description: Manually spread transactions across different batches.
Automate batch spreading with a custom script.
Randomly delay transactions by variable time intervals.
Use a tool to schedule transactions across batches automatically.

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring L2 anonymity set degradation across all heuristics compared to L1 baseline

methodology.step_1: Deploy test privacy transactions on Optimism and Arbitrum testnets with controlled deposit/withdrawal patterns
methodology.step_2: Record all L2 blocks, batch postings, and sequencer behavior during test period
methodology.step_3: Run all 5 heuristics against each deposit-withdrawal pair
methodology.step_4: Compare adversary success rate on L2 vs equivalent transactions on L1
methodology.step_5: Apply recommendations (timing delays, pool monitoring, batch separation) and re-measure
methodology.step_6: Quantify anonymity set size improvement from each recommendation

combined_benchmark.target: Recommendations should bring L2 anonymity set quality to within 2x of L1 baseline for same privacy protocol

adversary_levels.name: external_observer
adversary_levels.description: Can only see L1 batch postings and L2 public state

adversary_levels.name: rpc_provider
adversary_levels.description: Sees RPC requests and can correlate IP to transactions

adversary_levels.name: sequencer_level
adversary_levels.description: Full sequencer visibility including pre-confirmation ordering

templates.risk_assessment_block: BLOCKED: {heuristic_name} detected with {confidence}% confidence. {signal_description}. L2 anonymity set is insufficient for this transaction.
templates.summary: Analyzed {n_txs} L2 privacy transactions. {n_risky} flagged ({pct_risky}%). Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.summary_clean: All {n_txs} L2 transactions pass anonymity set checks. Pool sizes and timing are within acceptable parameters.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}, I can run {skill_action}. This improves your anonymity set by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
templates.anonymity_set_report: L2 anonymity set: {anon_set} depositors in your bracket (last 24h). L1 equivalent: {l1_anon_set}. Ratio: {ratio}x.
templates.timing_warning: L2 block time is {block_time}s. Your {dwell_time}s dwell spans {n_blocks} blocks -- adversary has {resolution}x more timing resolution than on L1.
